text: robotic assisted ventral hernia repair with primary closure and mesh placement by Dr. Andrew Iraniha.

This is a fifty-five year old female with a BMI of thirty-two who has had a very large supraumbilical ventral hernia. with a large bulge in the supraumbilical region with on and off pain and discomfort during activity and straining.
The patient is positioned supine on the operating table with her left arm tucked in, while the robotic surgical system is positioned on the right side of the patient.

The surgeon reduces the incarcerated omentum from the hernia defect. The hernia defect is then closed primarily using a number O V-lock non-absorbable suture in a simple running fashion, with reconstruction of the oblocus during the closure.
A 10 by 15 inch mesh is placed into the abdomen and secured to the abdominal wall using two OV lock absorbable sutures in a simple running fashion.
The surgeon secures the center of the mesh to the abdominal wall using U-interrupted sutures with 2-0 Ethibond, completing the mesh fixation process.

This procedure was performed as an outpatient. She required narcotics for a few days after the surgery and was able to perform her regular activity and went back to work two weeks after the operation.
